name: Direct Use of Unsafe JNI
description: A Java application directly uses JNI (Java Native Interface) to call native C/C++ code, bypassing Java's memory safety protections and exposing the application to buffer overflows, use-after-free, and other native code vulnerabilities.
detection_pattern: STRUCTURAL[java_class] contains JNI native method declarations that bridge to EXTERNAL[native_code] written in C/C++ without memory safety guarantees, creating a path where INGRESS[java_data] crosses into unsafe native code that lacks bounds checking, type safety, or memory management protections
